fields.description:
## Context
DSO plugin maintainers and skill authors committing code are blocked by the pre-commit test gate requiring irrelevant tests (false positives from fuzzy-match), and separately surprised by CI failures for tests the gate didn't require (false negatives from missed associations). There is no way to see which mappings the gate enforced, no feedback loop when CI catches a gap, and no tool to audit mapping quality. At least 3 merge-state bugs historically required multi-file patches partly because mapping gaps weren't surfaced early.

## Success Criteria
1. Per-commit structured logging shows which source files triggered which test requirements, including the match method (fuzzy vs .test-index), written as key=value lines to $ARTIFACTS_DIR/test-gate-audit.log (configurable via dso-config.conf test_gate.audit_log_path) — zero new I/O operations beyond appending in the existing gate loop
2. A periodic mapping quality analysis tool flags "phantom mappings" — test files that fuzzy-match a source file by name but contain no import, source, or function call referencing symbols from the mapped source file (verified via AST-level analysis for Python, grep-level source/function-name analysis for bash)
3. When CI catches a test failure that the commit gate did not require, a post-failure script identifies the source-test association and appends a suggested .test-index entry to .test-index-suggestions — deduplicating against existing .test-index and .test-index-suggestions entries before writing. When multiple source files changed, the script emits one suggestion per (source, failing-test) pair where the test name fuzzy-matches the source; ambiguous cases are omitted rather than guessed
4. .test-index-suggestions uses the same one-line-per-entry format as .test-index (source/path.ext: test/path.ext), keyed by source path, so git merges cleanly when different sources are added by concurrent branches. The file is created on first write if absent (no failure on fresh clone)
5. Sprint init (sprint-list-epics.sh or session start) surfaces pending .test-index-suggestions entries to the maintainer before work begins, enabling the feedback loop: CI failure -> suggestion -> applied -> gate catches it next time
6. In a repo with 5 known phantom mappings seeded in .test-index, the analysis tool identifies all 5; a manual audit of the first 10 CI-generated suggestions confirms zero false suggestions — this validates both the phantom detection accuracy and the CI feedback loop quality

## Scenario Analysis
- Log file grows unbounded on high-commit-frequency repos (high, runtime)
- Concurrent commits corrupt structured log without file locking (medium, runtime)
- Post-failure script misattributes CI failure with multiple changed files (medium, runtime) — addressed by SC3 multi-file rule
- False phantom-mapping reports after source file renames (high, runtime)
- Post-failure script not wired into CI on first deploy, silently never runs (medium, deployment)
- Log path differs between local and CI environments (high, deployment) — addressed by SC1 configurable path
- Sprint-init blocks on hundreds of accumulated suggestions (medium, deployment)
- .test-index-suggestions absent on fresh clone breaks post-failure script (medium, deployment) — addressed by SC4 create-on-first-write
- Fuzzy match threshold misconfiguration flags all mappings as phantoms (high, configuration)
- Log rotation/path not configurable (medium, configuration) — addressed by SC1 configurable path
- REBASE_HEAD filter not applied to post-failure script (medium, configuration)

## Dependencies
None. Relates to 1a5b-35f9 (Centrality-Aware Test Gate) — both touch record-test-status.sh and JSONL logging infrastructure. Coordinate logging schema or sequence this epic after 1a5b-35f9 to avoid merge conflicts.

## Approach
Log-and-Suggest Pipeline: The test gate emits lightweight structured log lines per mapping decision (zero commit-time overhead). A separate analyze-test-gate-log.sh reads logs offline to surface phantom and stale mappings via static analysis. On CI failure, a post-failure script parses failing tests against changed files and outputs suggested .test-index entries to .test-index-suggestions. Suggestions are deduped on write and keyed by source path for merge safety. All analysis happens outside the commit path.
